exercises.name: BOSU Squat to Press
exercises.description:
Stand on a BOSU ball while holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder level. 

Keep your core engaged and balance steady as you lower into a squat. As you rise back up, press the dumbbells overhead until your arms are fully extended.

Lower the dumbbells back to shoulder level and repeat.

exercises.name: BOSU V-Sit to Twist
exercises.description:
Sit on a BOSU ball with the dome side up, balancing on your sit bones. 

Lift your feet off the floor and extend your legs slightly to form a V-sit position. Hold your hands together in front of your chest and rotate your torso to one side, then twist to the opposite side in a controlled motion. 

Continue twisting side to side while keeping your core engaged and balance steady.

exercises.name: Box Assisted Muscle ups
exercises.description:
1. Stand on a box directly under a pull-up bar. Place your hands on the bar about shoulder-width apart and crouch down on the box. 

2. Jump upwards using your momentum as you pull the bar towards your torso, bringing yourself as high as possible. 

3. Once the bar contacts your body, lean forward and rotate the elbows upwards. 

4. Push yourself upwards away from the bar, until your arms are fully locked and you are over top of the bar.

exercises.name: Box Jump
exercises.description:
1) When performing a box jump, try to drive from your hips as much as possible. 

2) Swing with your arms to give momentum. 

3) If you choose to jump down rather than step down from the box, be sure to land in a soft squat.

exercises.name: Box Jump to Dumbbell Thruster
exercises.description:
Stand facing a box holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height. 

Perform an explosive jump onto the box, landing softly in a squat position. 

Immediately press the dumbbells overhead into a thruster, then step or jump down from the box and repeat the sequence.

exercises.name: Box Jump to Squat Hold
exercises.description:
From a standing position facing a box, leap forward onto it with both feet, landing softly in a squat position. 

Hold the squat briefly to stabilize, then step or jump back down to the floor and repeat the movement.

exercises.name: Box Pistol Squat
exercises.description:
1.Stand upright.

2.Lower your body towards the box, sending your hips back and down, raising one leg, arms extended in front.

3.Push off the other leg to return to starting position.

4. Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Box Step-Off to Broad Jump
exercises.description: Step off a box and explode into a broad jump forward.

exercises.name: Box Step-Off to Lateral Bound
exercises.description: Step off a box and bound laterally to land on one foot.

exercises.name: Box Step-Off to Single-Leg Squat
exercises.description: Step off a box and balance on one leg while lowering into a squat.

exercises.name: Box Step-Off to Squat
exercises.description: Step off a box and immediately land into a controlled squat.

exercises.name: Box Step-Off to Vertical Jump
exercises.description: Step off a box and jump straight up, focusing on height.

exercises.name: Bracknell Gym | Kinesis High Pull Station exercises
exercises.description: Bracknell Gym | Kinesis High Pull Station exercises

exercises.name: Broad Jump
exercises.description: Jump forward with both feet together, landing softly and immediately jumping back into the next rep. Focus on explosive power.

exercises.name: Broad Jump to Backward Shuffle
exercises.description:
Start standing tall with feet hip-width apart. Perform an explosive broad jump forward, landing softly with knees slightly bent. 

Immediately transition into a backward shuffle, moving quickly in a controlled manner while maintaining a low, athletic stance.

exercises.name: B-stance Dumbbell RDL
exercises.description: B-stance Dumbbell RDL

exercises.name: B-Stance RDL Single Dumbbell
exercises.description:
Stand tall holding a single dumbbell in one hand on the side opposite the forward leg. Position one foot slightly forward and the other slightly back (B‑stance), keeping most of your weight on the front leg. 

Hinge at the hips and lower the dumbbell toward the floor, maintaining a slight bend in your knees and a straight back. Engage your glutes and hamstrings to return to standing.

exercises.name: Build Inner Chest-Close Dumbbell Hammer Press
exercises.description: Build Inner Chest-Close Dumbbell Hammer Press

exercises.name: Bulgarian drop sets
exercises.description: Make sure to use something to elevate front foot slightly if bench or box is tall. Start with 20-15 lbs do amrap , drop downs to 5 lbs and AMRAP, then no weight AMRAP

exercises.name: Bulgarian Pulses
exercises.description:
Stand a few feet in front of a bench or elevated surface and place one foot behind you on the bench with your toes resting on it. 

Lower your body into a split squat position until your front thigh is nearly parallel to the floor, keeping your chest up and core engaged. From the bottom position, perform small controlled pulses up and down without fully standing up. 

Keep tension in your front leg throughout the movement.

exercises.name: Bulgarian Split Squat with Rotation
exercises.description: Bulgarian Split Squat with Rotation

exercises.name: Burpee
exercises.description:
1.Start in the top of a push up position.

2.Jump your feet in, bringing your knees to your chest while keeping your hands on the floor.

3.Come to an upright position and jump into the air, raising your arms overhead.
 
4.Return to the mid position and then jump your feet back out to a straight position.

exercises.name: Burpee Broad Jump
exercises.description: Perform a burpee, then explode into a forward broad jump before resetting.

exercises.name: Burpee Pull Ups
exercises.description:
1. Place your hands on the floor, jumping feet backwards, and lower your chest to the floor. 

2. Push yourself back up to a standing position, then jump upwards to grab a pull up bar. 

3. Immediately pull yourself upwards until your chin is above the bar. Lower yourself and repeat.

exercises.name: Burpee to Target
exercises.description:
1. Place your hands on the floor, jumping feet backwards, and lowering your chest to the floor. 

2. Push yourself back up to a standing position, then jump upwards to touch an overhead target with both of your hands.

exercises.name: Burpee to Tuck Jump
exercises.description:
Start standing tall with your feet shoulder-width apart and your hands at your sides. Begin the movement by explosively jumping upward with your hands reaching overhead. 

Upon landing, immediately lower into a squat, place your hands on the floor, and jump your feet back into a high plank position. Perform a push-up, then jump your feet forward toward your hands and rise back up to standing. 

From there, explode into a tuck jump by driving your knees toward your chest. Land softly and go right into the next repetition.

exercises.name: Butt Kickers
exercises.description:
1. Stand with your legs shoulder-width apart. Your arms should be bent.

2. Flex the right knee and kick your right heel up toward your glutes. Bring the right foot back down.

3. Try to keep your knees in line with your body as best as you can.

4. As the right leg comes down, flex your left knee and kick your left foot up toward your glutes. 

5. Repeat.

exercises.name: Butterfly Pull Up
exercises.description:
1. Start by gripping a pull up bar with a grip slightly wider than shoulder-width. 

2. Initiate the movement with the shoulders, pushing your body into an arched back position. 

3. From the arched position, lift your hips and legs straight up towards the bar, pulling yourself upwards at the same time. 

4. Pull yourself up until your chin is above the bar. Maintain the arched position with your torso, allowing the body to travel forwards before kipping for the next rep.

exercises.name: BW circuit push-up/alt knee / toe tap
Cable Bent Over Chest Fly
exercises.description:
1.Bend over at the waist holding handles with your arms straight out to your sides at shoulder height and your feet split.

2.Pull the handles down together in front until they meet, keeping your arms straight.

3.Return to starting position.

4.Repeat.
